prompt.text: Hmm, are you having some trouble? Explain to each other how you understand the task and think of how you could move forward together.
Good discussions! How could you together gain a better understanding of your current task?
Great reflecting! How could you still support each other so that you could progress as a group?
Try to still jointly discuss how your current approach is beneficial for this task. This will help you get past a difficult situation.
There seems to be some confusion in the air. Explain to each other what you are trying to understand right now.
While listening to you, I wondered if you all understand the key concepts of the task. What could you do to reach a shared understanding of the task’s concepts?
Make sure that you have the needed information on the case to complete this task. Share ideas: what could help you reach a common understanding?
Wait! I noticed that not everyone is necessarily participating in this task. How could you make sure everyone gets to participate?
Hmm – has everyone had a chance to have their voice heard?
Let’s think for a moment – what does everyone think about their own participation so far?
How could you change your current way of working to ensure that everyone can participate?
It seems emotions are running a bit high. How could you talk to each other in a friendlier manner?
Cooperation is sometimes challenging! How can you make sure everyone can still participate in good spirits?
Every member is needed to form a functioning group. How could you better support each other?
Take a moment to think – how could you express your thoughts in a way that is not hurtful to others.
Well done on developing your thoughts! But are you still thinking about this from your own perspectives? Think how you could progress as a group.
I noticed that you might be thinking about this from individual points of view. What could be your joint plan for solving the task?
Could I help? Rethink what you are trying to accomplish here as a group. By doing this, you can support each other and move on.
